subtitle.problem: You've spent months playing with ChatGPT and Claude. Copying prompts. Testing tools. But you have nothing in production.
subtitle.solution: Agentcamp takes you from experimenting to operating in 4 weeks.

title.line2: It's the gap between demo and production.

0.title: Scattered Prompts
0.description: Every conversation starts from scratch. No system. No memory. No flow.

1.title: Demos That Die
1.description: It works on your laptop. You show it excitedly. It never makes it to production.

2.title: Zero Control
2.description: The agent hallucinates. Does random things. And you have no idea how to debug it.

explanation.problem: Most courses teach: "build an agent, then try to control it."
explanation.details: You start with workflows and state management. Agent autonomy comes later—within boundaries you define.
explanation.reality: This is how production teams actually build.

2.title: Real-World Integration
2.tagline: "Agents that connect with the real world"

supportText.title: Don't know how to code? No problem.
supportText.description:
You use AI coding assistants (Cursor, Claude Code) to generate code.
You direct. The AI writes. We teach you to orchestrate.

ecosystem.title: Production Infrastructure
ecosystem.subtitle: Agentcamp runs on the same stack used by Silicon Valley teams.
ecosystem.authority: Part of the Frutero ecosystem—LATAM's most active builder community.

0.quote: I spent 6 months playing with GPT. In 4 weeks I had an agent that processes my emails automatically.
0.name: María González

1.quote: The orchestration-first approach changed how I think about automation. Now everything has control.
1.name: Carlos Ruiz

2.quote: I thought I needed a developer. Agentcamp showed me I don't.
2.name: Ana López

items.0: You're a founder, marketer, ops lead, or creator
items.1: You've used ChatGPT/Claude for months but have nothing in production
items.2: You want automated systems, not scattered prompts
items.3: You have 5-7 hours per week to dedicate
items.4: You're ready to build, not just consume content

items.0: You're looking for a theoretical machine learning course
items.1: You expect AI to do everything without your supervision
items.2: You can't commit the weekly time
items.3: You're already a senior developer looking for advanced content
items.4: You want results without doing the work

price.note: Valued at $499 — free for the first cohort

pricing.guarantee: Week 1 Guarantee: If after the first week you feel it's not for you, just let us know. No questions asked.

0.question: Do I need to know how to code?
0.answer:
No. We use AI coding assistants (Cursor, Claude Code) to generate code. You learn to direct and orchestrate, not write Python from scratch.

Actual requirement: know how to use a computer and be willing to learn new tools.

1.question: How much time do I need to dedicate per week?
1.answer:
5-7 hours weekly:
• 3-4 hours in live sessions
• 2-3 hours of independent work and projects

The program is designed for people with full-time jobs.

2.question: What tools will I use?
2.answer:
The stack includes:
• IDE with AI assistant (Cursor or VS Code + Copilot)
• Agent framework (defined with sponsors)
• Claude API for LLM calls
• Composio for integrations
• Cloud platform for deployment

Everything is set up in pre-work before starting.

3.question: Are the sessions live or recorded?
3.answer: Live with interactive Q&A. Recordings are available for review, but the value is in participating live.

4.question: What if I fall behind?
4.answer:
You have office hours 2x per week to catch up. Async support in the community for specific questions. Access to recordings for review.

The program has a pace, but there's a support network.

5.question: What kind of agents can I build after?
5.answer:
The program gives you the foundation to build:
• Email and calendar assistants
• Automated content pipelines
• Research and analysis agents
• Customer support workflows
• Internal operational automations

The limit is your use case, not the technical knowledge.

6.question: Is there a community after the program?
6.answer: Yes. Permanent access to the Agentcamp community. Networking with other graduates. Content updates.

7.question: Why is it free?
7.answer: The program is sponsored by AI infrastructure companies that want more builders in LATAM to learn to build with their technology. You learn for free, they gain adoption. Win-win.

finalCta.subtitle: In 4 weeks you'll have an AI agent in production working for you—not another folder of prompts.
